snapshot.scope: Independent review of early Draft PR delivery, bounded review deltas, evidence reuse, proportional identity and repair gates, soft ownership-slice limits, stop conditions, and explicit context-routing feedback.

reviewers.independence: Product Planning reviews only contracts and executable governance; Development still owns every implementation deletion, rewrite, security test, package and runtime artifact.

dimensions.summary: One guide now routes coherent slices to Draft PRs early and keeps Ready/full-gate promotion behind owner clearance.
Role ownership, testing layers, documentation impact and handoff fields agree without changing CI or product contracts.
Requirement, Exit, Phase and semantic-claim projections remain unchanged because this candidate only changes development process.
Delta-only rereview, evidence reuse, proportional identity and soft scope triggers directly reduce pre-PR serialization.
The existing process owner is smaller after the update; no dashboard, new document, fixed round count, LOC gate or CI block is added.

verification.evidence: The compact process owner remains mapped, linked, contract-valid and within the repository documentation budget.
Existing Requirement, Exit, Phase and semantic-claim projections remain unchanged by the process-only update.
The full Planning suite passes after the reviewed snapshot refresh without CI, product or status changes.

coordination.compatibility: Current lightweight Draft CI and Ready/full-gate layering remain unchanged; feature and H3c delivery do not wait for this process candidate.
coordination.acknowledgement: Planning owns semantic binds, each owner reviews stable bytes once, and PM limits itself to scope, risk and publication decisions.

nonBlockingRisks: The path/domain thresholds are human review triggers rather than CI or LOC gates.
Cross-worktree replay, H-level security/release and observed drift still require stronger identity evidence.
